exemplar.title: To what extent did the right-wing party Law and Justice overhaul of the Polish media sphere undermine the system of democratic checks and balances in Poland from 2015 to 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the right-wing party Law and Justice overhaul of the Polish media sphere undermine the system of democratic checks and balances in Poland from 2015 to 2023?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation throughout.
Research question is clearly stated, sharply focused, and remains central to every section.
Section headings and transitions consistently link back to the overarching theme, demonstrating coherence.
weaknesses: Methodology is appropriate but lacks full justification of sourceâselection criteria and reliability assessment.
No primary data collection (e.g., interviews) was undertaken, limiting methodological depth.
Conclusion omits a discussion of methodological limitations and suggestions for future research.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic communication and research question) clearly meet the 5â6 band descriptors, showing accurate, sustained exposition and a sharply focused line of inquiry. The methodology strand sits at the top of the 3â4 band, but the overwhelming strength of the other two strands justifies a topâband overall score of 6 for Focus and Method.

strengths: Application of a broad mix of legal documents, NGO reports and academic texts in support of key arguments.
Coherent knowledge of democratic theory and Polish political developments is demonstrated.
Terminology such as âmedia pluralism,â âilliberal democracyâ and ârepolonisationâ is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Overreliance on Wikipedia and journalistic sources for conceptual definitions undermines academic rigor.
Several citations lack primary documents or full publication details, reducing traceability.
Some claims remain descriptive and are unsupported by primary evidence, limiting depth of understanding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands predominantly fall in the 3â4 band: sources are mostly relevant but inconsistently rigorous; knowledge is clear but not fully sophisticated; terminology is accurate but with occasional minor inaccuracies. A midâband score of 4 reflects solid performance tempered by these recurring weaknesses.

strengths: Research draws on an extensive, relevant bibliography (legal texts, NGO/EU reports, scholarly works) directly aligned to the research question.
Analysis consistently links legislation to democratic theory, offering wellâsupported causeâandâeffect arguments.
Empirical data (e.g., World Press Freedom Index, EU Media Freedom Act) are effectively integrated to underpin conclusions.
weaknesses: Counterâarguments from government or media owners are rarely explored, limiting balance.
Evaluation of evidence quality is often descriptive rather than deeply interrogative.
Sections such as Lex TVN and constitutional court rulings lack comparative legal analysis for greater critical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While primary evaluation is sometimes superficial and counterarguments are underexplored, the essay exhibits good, mostly wellâsupported research and analysis consistent with the 7â9 band. The coherent, focused argument and effective use of quantitative and theoretical evidence justify a high-band score of 9 for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Clear, logical structure with comprehensive table of contents and coherent section headings.
Consistent formatting of title page, abstracted word count, and pagination supports readability.
Paragraphing generally aids comprehension and follows the line of argument.
weaknesses: Citation style is inconsistently applied (mix of footnotes and hyperlinks, variable bibliography formatting).
Dense, overly long paragraphs impede readability and would benefit from division.
URLs and bibliography entries sometimes break across lines, disrupting functionality and flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all core structural requirements and supports understanding at a high level, aligning with the top of the 3â4 band. Minor layout and formatting inconsistencies prevent a perfect score, but the overall clarity and organization warrant the maximum Presentation mark of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Analysis shows clear lines of argument but remains uneven in depth.**  

â¢ The essay links legislative changes (e.g., 2015 media law, National Media Council, Orlen takeover) to democratic theory, offering cause-and-effect explanations of how control over appointments, funding and ownership weakens checks and balances.  
â¢ Evidence is generally marshalled to support claims, yet some assertions (e.g., causal links between media reform and 2023 election defeat) are stated rather than thoroughly substantiated. Counter-arguments are acknowledged but not explored in detail, and quantitative data are limited to a few indices.  

Consequently, the work demonstrates **adequate-to-good analytical engagement with partial support**, aligning with the **4â6 band** (mid-level score awarded).

**Research quality is generally sound and aligned with the RQ.**  

â¢ The bibliography is extensive (30+ items) and includes legal documents, NGO reports, EU directives and scholarly works, indicating a **broad and largely relevant evidence base**.  
â¢ The sources selected relate directly to the Polish media environment and democratic theory, demonstrating deliberate connection to the research focus.  
â¢ Nonetheless, the inclusion of tertiary sources (e.g., Wikipedia) and limited critical assessment of credibility reveal some shortcomings, preventing classification as âconsistently excellent.â  

These characteristics fit comfortably in the **4â6 band**, meriting a midpoint score.

**Logical and reader-friendly structure.**  

â¢ The essay has an accurate title page, abstracted word count, complete table of contents, clear section headings that mirror the line of argument and a properly ordered bibliography.  
â¢ Paragraphing is coherent and facilitates understanding; each stage of the investigation is separated and sequenced.  

Minor issues (occasional uneven paragraph lengths, some redundancy) do not impede clarity, so the work meets the **3â4 band**, awarded the higher mark.

**Sound but not fully sophisticated understanding of the topic and discipline.**  

â¢ The student demonstrates a clear grasp of democratic theory (polyarchy, illiberal democracy, liberalism) and Polish political developments, using them to frame the argument.  
â¢ There is reasonable synthesis of historical events, constitutional principles and EU norms, indicating coherent knowledge. Nevertheless, parts of the narrative remain descriptive, and deeper theoretical engagement (e.g., with media-state relations in global politics scholarship) is limited.  

Hence the work fits securely within the **3â4 band**: clear knowledge with partially effectiveâbut not consistently penetratingâuse of sources.

**Discussion is coherent but the depth of critical evaluation is moderate.**  

â¢ The student constructs a logical narrative showing progressive erosion of media independence, culminating in an evaluative conclusion that PiS policies contradict their own stated aims and democratic theory.  
â¢ Evaluation of differing perspectives (government vs. opposition, EU bodies) is present but often descriptive; the essay seldom interrogates evidence quality or present alternative explanations in depth.  
â¢ Reflection on limitations appears mainly in the methodology rather than within the main argument, and synthesis across sections could be stronger.  

These traits correspond to the **4â6 band**, though on the lower side; hence a score of 4 is appropriate.

**Most formal presentation requirements are met, with minor lapses.**  

â¢ Standard font, spacing, pagination, a labelled table of contents and consistent in-text citations are provided, fulfilling core layout expectations.  
â¢ However, citation style is inconsistently applied (mix of footnotes and hyperlinks, variable bibliographic formatting), and some web addresses break over lines unsystematically. These lapses suggest that conventions were followed but not always *correctly* or *consistently*.

Given that layout considerations are present yet imperfect, the strand sits in the **3â4 band**, but at the lower end with a score of 3.

**Terminology is generally accurate and appropriate but not flawless.**  

â¢ Key political science termsââmedia pluralism,â âchecks and balances,â âilliberal democracy,â âpolyarchy,â ârepolonisation,â etc.âare employed correctly and usually defined.  
â¢ Occasional awkward phrasing and minor inaccuracies (e.g., conflating âmedia democracyâ with âmedia pluralismâ) show that command of specialist vocabulary, while solid, is not fully consistent.  

The performance therefore meets the **3â4 band** descriptor of mostly accurate use of terminology and concepts.

**Clear statement and sustained exposition of the topic**: From the essayâs title through the opening paragraphs, the student consistently defines key ideasâmedia pluralism, democratic checks-and-balances and the Polish contextâshowing an assured command of the material. 

â¢ The introduction explains why the media are considered a âfourth authority,â cites internationally recognised indicators (e.g., RSF Press Freedom Index) and situates the Polish case within wider democratic theory, fulfilling the requirement for comprehensive explanation.  
â¢ Throughout the body, each section heading (âFirst stage â reformation of public media,â âRepolonisation,â etc.) explicitly links back to the overarching theme of how PiS reforms affected democratic oversight. 

Because the topic is communicated **accurately, coherently and in depth** across the essay, the work fits the 5â6 band.

**Methodological planning is serviceable but not fully robust.**  

â¢ The student identifies secondary-source analysis as the main method, outlines use of government documents, NGO reports, academic works and journalistic articles, and explains levels of analysis (national, international, local) and a liberalism theoretical lens. This shows **some informed selection and relevance** of sources/methods.  
â¢ However, the methodology is limited to desk research; no primary data (e.g., interviews) are collected despite their potential value, and criteria for source selection or evaluation of reliability are noted only briefly. Citations include several Wikipedia pages, suggesting uneven rigour.  

These features align with the **3â4 band**: methodology is mostly complete and relevant but lacks the comprehensive, clearly justified selection expected for the top band.

**Research question quality**: âTo what extent did the right-wing party Law and Justice overhaul of the Polish media sphere undermine the system of democratic checks and balances in Poland from 2015 to 2023?â is precise in time, actor and concept.  

â¢ The wording directs the investigation toward measurable democratic safeguards (media pluralism, institutional independence, funding control, etc.), giving a *clear line of inquiry* that frames all subsequent sections.  
â¢ Each chapter of the essay (public-service reform, ârepolonisation,â Lex TVN) maps directly onto the RQ, demonstrating a **sharp and sustained focus**.  

Consequently, the strand meets the descriptor for the **5â6 band**: the RQ is clearly stated, focused and remains central to the discussion.

**Generally appropriate use of sources with occasional weaknesses.**  

â¢ The essay draws on a broad mix of legal documents, NGO and EU reports, parliamentary records and academic texts, normally integrating them to support points about media control, funding and ownership.  
â¢ Source material is mostly relevant, and quotations are cited; however, reliance on journalistic outlets and Wikipedia for conceptual definitions detracts from academic rigour, and some claims remain unsupported by primary evidence.  

Overall, the application is **mostly relevant and appropriate but not consistently rigorous**, corresponding to the **3â4 band**.

annotations.comment: The conclusion paragraph is cut off, leaving the final point incomplete; review and ensure full proofing for a coherent closing.
The transition into the financing section is clear, linking legislative control to budgetary allocations and reinforcing the research focus.
Presenting the funding data in a narrative obscures trends; including a table or chart would enhance clarity and allow for visual comparison over time.
Budget figures are cited from secondary reports but lack primary government financial documents; referencing official budget statements would improve source credibility.
The repolonisation narrative is informative but would benefit from closer legal comparison of relevant constitutional provisions and precedents.
Dense, long paragraphs impede readability; break this discussion into shorter paragraphs focused on single points to enhance engagement.
Interviews by HFHR provide valuable primary insight, but the analysis remains largely descriptive; apply thematic coding or triangulate with quantitative data for deeper evaluation.
No counter-arguments from government or media owners are explored; including official responses would balance the evaluation and strengthen credibility.
The Lex TVN section outlines the draft law well but omits analysis of its alignment with EU media directives; a comparison would deepen critical insight.
Long legal quotations disrupt pacing; summarizing key provisions in your own words will maintain focus and reduce reader fatigue.
Linking the case study to the EUâs European Media Freedom Act demonstrates excellent synthesis of national events within broader supranational developments.
The final summary is concise but omits reflection on limitations and suggestions for future research, which are essential for a focused and methodologically transparent conclusion.
The research question is stated precisely, specifying actor, timeframe and concepts, providing a clear and sharply focused line of inquiry that underpins the entire investigation.
Using the World Press Freedom Index data to frame the research question provides a strong empirical basis and demonstrates effective use of quantitative evidence.
The methodology description lacks criteria for source selection and evaluation of reliability; specify how sources were chosen and assessed to justify their inclusion.
Acknowledging biases and limitations encountered in source selection shows critical self-reflection and enhances the trustworthiness of your research.
Anchoring your analysis in liberalism theory provides a coherent conceptual lens and demonstrates effective use of disciplinary concepts.
The description of media-law amendments is detailed but lacks analysis of the political motivations and strategic objectives behind these reforms.
The constitutional courtâs ruling is cited but not interrogated; a deeper evaluation of the legal arguments would strengthen the critical discussion.
Reliance on Wikipedia for legislative and institutional history weakens academic rigor; replace tertiary sources with primary legal texts or peer-reviewed analyses.
The structure of appointments to the National Media Council is complex; consider adding a simple diagram or table to clarify its composition and voting dynamics.
Lengthy footnotes disrupt the flow; integrate essential background from footnotes into the main text and reserve footnotes for secondary details.
Recommendations would be strengthened by comparative examples from other democracies, illustrating how similar councils operate with cross-party legitimacy.
Conclusion paragraphs are lengthy and cover distinct themes; subdividing them into titled subsections would improve clarity and reader navigation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and mirrors the structure of the argument, guiding the reader clearly through each section of the essay.
The introduction relies heavily on popular sources (e.g., The Tribune Journal) and lacks critical evaluation of their academic reliability, which may weaken the theoretical foundation.
Citation formatting is inconsistent, mixing footnotes and in-text references; adopting a single, coherent style would improve readability and professionalism.
The paragraph on Dahl ends abruptly mid-sentence, disrupting coherence; ensure each theoretical concept is fully articulated and connected to your argument.
Bibliography numbering restarts and is disjointed in later pages; adopting a continuous numbering sequence will improve navigability.
The conclusion offers concrete, well-justified recommendations that clearly derive from the analysis, reflecting strong critical engagement.
Several URLs in the reference list break across lines, compromising hyperlink functionality; ensure links remain intact for easy access.
Several stenographic reports are cited without publisher or date details, reducing traceability; include full source information (publisher, date) for completeness.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent citation styles and formatting; standardizing layout (e.g., italics, punctuation) will enhance overall presentation.
Excessive reliance on Wikipedia in the bibliography undercuts academic rigor; replace tertiary entries with peer-reviewed or primary legal sources.
